2007-06-01.title: Ambush Kills 16 Policemen on Highway in Afghanistan

2007-06-01.title: Big, Gritty Chongqing, City of 12 Million, Is China’s Model for Future
2007-06-01.abstract: China’s rush to build megacities is part of a major push to spread China’s economic boom to the vast interior.

2007-06-01.title: Sunni Fighters Battle Al Qaeda in Baghdad
2007-06-01.abstract: It was unclear whether the fighting was driven by differing ideologies or was a battle for control of an area.

2007-06-01.title: Bush Proposes Goals on Greenhouse Gas Emissions
2007-06-01.abstract: Such an agreement would be a major shift for a White House that has resisted setting firm limits on emissions.

2007-06-01.title: Rockets Fray Nerves in Israeli ‘Bull’s-Eye’ City
2007-06-01.abstract: After seven years of rocket fire, sometimes sporadic and sometimes intense, Sderot, a working-class Israeli town, has turned into a city of fear.

2007-06-02.title: A Journey to, and From, the Heart of Radical Islam in Britain
2007-06-02.abstract: Ed Husain’s recently published memoir, “The Islamist, Why I Joined Radical Islam in Britain, What I Saw Inside and Why I Left,” has caused a ruckus.

2007-06-02.title: Number of Unidentified Bodies Found in Baghdad Rose Sharply in May
2007-06-02.abstract: The figures indicate that sectarian killings are rising sharply as militias return to the streets after lying low during the first few months of the troop “surge.”

2007-06-02.title: 2 American Pilots Are Indicted in Brazilian Airliner Crash
2007-06-02.abstract: The Brazilian Federal Police said that the two Americans had not knowingly caused the crash, but that their negligence had contributed to Brazil’s worst air disaster.

2007-06-02.title: Seeking Trade to Bridge a Political North-South Divide
2007-06-02.abstract: The nearly completed bridge over the Panj River is at the center of plans to connect trade and energy markets in South and Central Asia.

2007-06-02.title: Bush’s Greenhouse Gas Plan Throws Europe Off Guard
2007-06-02.abstract: President Bush’s vaguely worded proposal has muddied what had been shaping up as a black-and-white showdown between Europe and the United States.

2007-06-03.title: With Korea as Model, Bush Team Ponders Long Support Role in Iraq
2007-06-03.abstract: For the first time, the Bush administration is publicly discussing basing American troops in Iraq for years.

2007-06-03.title: Investigator Faults Colonel for Actions During Killings
2007-06-03.abstract: A Marine investigating officer implicitly criticized a commander’s decisions to remain miles away during a battle with insurgents in and around Haditha, Iraq.

2007-06-03.title: Fighting in Northern Lebanese Camp Spreads
2007-06-03.abstract: The fighting increases fears that the battle between the army and Islamist militants could spread even further.

2007-06-03.title: Bridge Bombing Increases Tensions in Northern Iraq
2007-06-03.abstract: Bomb blasts severely damaged a bridge linking Baghdad with the northern city of Kirkuk, heightening tensions between Arabs and Kurds.

2007-06-03.title: In a New India, an Old Industry Buoys Peasants
2007-06-03.abstract: Migrants who can no longer live by farming are flocking to brickyards to fuel the Indian construction boom.

2007-06-04.title: Somalia’s Prime Minister Survives Suicide Attack; 7 Others Die
2007-06-04.abstract: A suicide bomber rammed a pickup truck packed with explosives into the gates of the prime minister’s house.

2007-06-04.title: Bush Climate Plan: Amid Nays, Some Maybes
2007-06-04.abstract: President Bush’s new proposal is a potentially useful tool for gaining consensus on cutting worldwide emissions linked to global warming.

2007-06-04.title: Hamas Militant Say They Shelled Border Crossing
2007-06-04.abstract: The shelling occurred hours after Israeli forces shot and killed a Palestinian man in the West Bank city of Jenin.

2007-06-04.title: Gates Cautious About Gains in Afghanistan
2007-06-04.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates expressed guarded optimism about the progress of the military campaign against a resurgent Taliban.

2007-06-04.title: Gates Says Iranian Weapons Flowing Into Afghanistan
2007-06-04.abstract: The defense secretary said that he and the president of Afghanistan agreed that there was no direct evidence that the Iranian government was behind the arms shipments.

2007-06-05.title: Leaving the Chaos Behind
2007-06-05.abstract: They began college with dreams of parties and friends. But four years later, Iraq's college graduates are eager to flee their country.

2007-06-05.title: 9 Arrested in Plan to Oust Laos Government
2007-06-05.abstract: American officials said the men planned to attack government buildings in the Laotian capital, Vientiane, and elsewhere.

2007-06-05.title: Basque Group Ends Ceasefire
2007-06-05.abstract: ETA, the armed Basque separatist group, today vowed to resume its violent struggle for an independent nation.

2007-06-05.title: Blair Calls on Moderates to Reclaim the Public Debate Over Islam
2007-06-05.abstract: The Prime Minister, who has said he will take a special interest in interfaith affairs when he leaves office, said the true meaning of Islam had been hijacked by extremists.

2007-06-05.title: Gates Says Arms Go to Taliban, but Iran’s Role Is Not Certain
2007-06-05.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates said it was unclear whether Iran’s government was behind the arms shipments.

2007-06-06.title: Protestors Keep Tensions High at Summit
2007-06-06.abstract: KÜHLUNGSBORN, Germany, June 6 — They lurked in the woods, fifty strong, wearing black hoods and bandannas, and wielding tree limbs. At a signal given by one of their number, they sprang forward and threw their debris across a road here, creating a barricade that brought traffic to a screeching halt.

2007-06-06.title: A Vestige of British Rule Is About to Vanish, Unmourned
2007-06-06.abstract: As Hong Kong prepares for the 10th anniversary of its return to Chinese rule, Queen’s Pier has emerged as one of several symbols of Britain’s disappearing influence.

2007-06-06.title: New AIDS Cases in Africa Outpace Treatment Gains
2007-06-06.abstract: For each sub-Saharan African who was placed on anti-AIDS drugs last year, experts say, five more were newly infected.

2007-06-06.title: Cyclone Nears Iran and Oman
2007-06-06.abstract: Oman and Iran girded for heavy flooding and winds as the strongest cyclone ever recorded by scientists in the northwestern Arabian Sea neared.

2007-06-06.title: Abbas Pulls Out of  Talks With Israel
2007-06-06.abstract: The Palestinian president, Mahmoud Abbas, dropped out after the two sides could not agree on an agenda.

2007-06-07.title: At Group of 8 Meeting, Bush Rebuffs Germany on Cutting Emissions
2007-06-07.abstract: The White House said it would hold firm against concrete long-term targets for reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

2007-06-07.title: Bombs Hit Shiites in Baghdad; Turkish Threats Grow Acute
2007-06-07.abstract: Reports emerged late in the day suggesting that Turkish forces had begun a long-threatened incursion into northern Iraq to hunt Kurdish guerrillas.

2007-06-07.title: Calmer Talk From U.S. and Russia
2007-06-07.abstract: Russia and the United States on Wednesday appeared to step back from their confrontation over American plans for installations in Europe relating to a missile shield.

2007-06-07.title: India Has Fewer AIDS Victims Than Thought
2007-06-07.abstract: If the results of a survey are correct, India is no longer the world’s supposed leader of people infected with AIDS.

2007-06-07.title: Ballot Boxes? Yes. Actual Democracy? Tough Question.
2007-06-07.abstract: Elections in the Middle East, it appears, have increasingly become a tool used by authoritarian leaders to claim legitimacy.

2007-06-08.title: Blair’s Accord With Libya Worries Kin From Lockerbie
2007-06-08.abstract: The accord might permit the only person convicted in the 1988 Lockerbie bombing, Britain’s bloodiest terrorist attack, to return to Libya to complete his sentence.

2007-06-08.title: Former Killing Ground Becomes Shrine to Stalin’s Victims
2007-06-08.abstract: Perhaps more than 20,000 people were shot and buried at the height of Stalin’s purges on the southern edge of Moscow.

2007-06-08.title: To Gain Among Muslims, Indonesia Offers to Mediate Middle East Disputes
2007-06-08.abstract: Indonesia, the world’s most populous Muslim nation, has signaled a desire to take a bigger role in solving problems in the Islamic world.

2007-06-08.title: Italy: Terrorism Suspects Held in Italy and Britain
2007-06-08.abstract: The authorities in Milan issued arrest warrants for nine men suspected of providing logistical and financial support to Al Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, North Africa’s most active terrorist group. By evening, at least two of the suspects were in Italian custody. British authorities made a separate arrest in North London as part of the sweep. The North African group, formerly the Salafist Group for Preaching and Combat, claimed responsibility for the suicide bombings in Algiers in April that killed 33 people.

2007-06-08.title: Gaza: One Dead in Factional Firefight
2007-06-08.abstract: A Palestinian man was killed and at least nine other people were wounded in factional fighting in the southern Gaza Strip town of Rafah. Each of the rival factions, Hamas and Fatah, blamed the other for starting the shooting. The dead man belonged to Fatah. A report this week by the Palestinian Independent Commission for Citizens’ Rights said 616 Palestinians had been killed in internal fighting since the beginning of 2006. In the first half of May, fierce factional clashes left about 50 dead in Gaza. The Palestinian president, Mahmoud Abbas of Fatah, warned this week that the Palestinians were “on the verge of civil war.”.

2007-06-09.title: Bush Meets Pope Benedict for the First Time
2007-06-09.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI expressed concerns to President Bush about the Middle East, according to a church statement.

2007-06-09.title: G-8 Leaders Reaffirm Promises of Billions to Battle AIDS and Other Diseases
2007-06-09.abstract: The $60 billion pledge, which is to extend over an unspecified number of years, includes aid already promised by the Group of 8 leaders.

2007-06-09.title: Putin Proposes Alternatives on Missile Defense
2007-06-09.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin proposed that Russia and the West study emerging missile threats jointly.

2007-06-09.title: Ivory Coast: Cocoa Fueled Civil War
2007-06-09.abstract: A report by an anti-corruption organization found that profits from smuggled and mishandled cocoa had helped pay for the civil war in Ivory Coast.

2007-06-09.title: Bush, Feeling Ill and Resting, Attracts Notice by Absence
2007-06-09.abstract: A counselor to President Bush reported that the president had awakened “not feeling well to the stomach.”

2007-06-10.title: Trinidad Group Denies Link to New York Bomb Plot
2007-06-10.abstract: Those who have studied Jamaat al Muslimeen consider it a stretch to mention it and Al Qaeda in the same breath.

2007-06-10.title: Israeli Air Force Strikes Back Against Palestinians
2007-06-10.abstract: The Israeli air force struck at least two buildings in Gaza City in the early hours this morning in an apparent response to a raid by Palestinian militants on Saturday.

2007-06-10.title: Palestinians Attempt to Capture Israeli Soldier
2007-06-10.abstract: The battle lasted two to three hours, but no Israeli was wounded or captured, the Israeli Army said.

2007-06-10.title: Corporate Korea Corks the Bottle as Women Rise
2007-06-10.abstract: As more women join the ranks, Korea’s male-centered corporate culture is changing, starting with alcohol.

2007-06-10.title: A War Ends in Ivory Coast but Peace, Order and Unity Are Flickering Dreams
2007-06-10.abstract: For most Ivorians, little has improved since rebels signed a peace pact in March ending a five-year war.

2007-06-11.title: Sri Lanka Human Rights Panel Is Criticized
2007-06-11.abstract: An international panel appointed by the government of Sri Lanka has issued a stark indictment of a presidential commission impaneled to investigate human rights violations.

2007-06-11.title: Heavy Rains Set Off Flooding in Southern China
2007-06-11.abstract: Floods have claimed at least 66 lives and forced nearly 600,000 people to be relocated, the state news media reported.

2007-06-11.title: Warning: Trains Coming. A Masterpiece Is at Risk.
2007-06-11.abstract: The architect in charge of efforts to complete Antonio Gaudí’s unfinished masterwork La Sagrada Familia says the building is threatened by a planned train tunnel.

2007-06-11.title: Factional Violence Continues in Gaza
2007-06-11.abstract: At least seven Palestinians were killed in the fighting between the rival Palestinian factions, Hamas and Fatah.

2007-06-11.title: Sporadic Fighting Continues in Lebanon Refugee Camp
2007-06-11.abstract: As a conflict between Lebanese soldiers and militants entered its fourth week, there was sporadic fighting at the Nahr al Bared Palestinian refugee camp.

2007-06-12.title: Central African Republic: Aid Worker Shot Dead
2007-06-12.abstract: A French aid worker with the group Doctors Without Borders was killed in a volley of gunfire in an area of this vast and lawless country that had recently suffered through a rebel attack and army reprisals. The worker, a 27-year-old logistics specialist named Elsa Serfass, was part of an assessment mission that was sent to evaluate the medical needs of displaced villagers, the latest victims of an increasingly violent conflict between rebels in the northwest of the country and government troops. Dozens of villages have been set on fire in the last 18 months as the army has tried to flush out the rebels, forcing nearly 300,000 to flee their homes.

2007-06-12.title: British Company to Investigate Its Own Deal With the Saudis
2007-06-12.abstract: Britain’s biggest weapons maker is planning to set up an independent commission following allegations about payments linked to an arms deal in the 1980s.

2007-06-12.title: Pakistan: Judge Gains Legal Ground
2007-06-12.abstract: The Supreme Court agreed to allow its suspended chief justice, Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry, to contest his removal by President Pervez Musharraf before the full court. Mr. Chaudhry, who had become increasingly independent, was suspended in March on the ground that he had misused his office. The dismissal set off a nationwide backlash that has created the greatest challenge to the rule of General Musharraf since he seized power in a coup in 1999.

2007-06-12.title: A Compass That Can Clash With Modern Life
2007-06-12.abstract: Religious and political leaders say that there is a crisis in Islam because too many religious edicts are being issued.

2007-06-12.title: Bush, on European Tour, Perfects the Art of Meet and Run
2007-06-12.abstract: Being on the road with President Bush invariably invokes comparisons to that old movie “If It’s Tuesday, This Must Be Belgium.”

2007-06-13.title: Providing a Welcome Mat
2007-06-13.abstract: Sodertalje, Sweden, a thriving town about 18 miles southwest of Stockholm, has become the haven of choice for many Iraqi Christians.

2007-06-13.title: Blair Compares News Media to ‘Feral Beast’ in Angry Parting Shot
2007-06-13.abstract: Tony Blair used one of his last speeches before he leaves office to settle scores with a press corps he holds responsible for hounding him.

2007-06-13.title: Attacks Escalate as Palestinians Fight for Power
2007-06-13.abstract: Hamas and Fatah sharply escalated their fight for supremacy in what is starting to look like a civil war.

2007-06-13.title: U.S. Inquiry Hampered by Iraq Violence, Investigators Say
2007-06-13.abstract: Naval investigators testified at a military hearing that  insurgent attacks complicated their inquiry into the killing of 24 Iraqi civilians in Haditha in 2005.

2007-06-13.title: Second U.S. Official Presses Iraqi Premier for Action
2007-06-13.abstract: The deputy secretary of state urged the Shiite-led government to complete political reforms intended to reconcile Iraq’s warring sects.

2007-06-14.title: Efforts to Avert Sectarian Reprisals After Shrine Attack
2007-06-14.abstract: Appeals for calm appeared to have headed off the risk of a new sectarian convulsion, at least for now.

2007-06-14.title: Southeast Asian Terrorist Leader Is Under Arrest
2007-06-14.abstract: Indonesian police said Abu Dujana led the military arm of Jemaah Islamiyah, blamed for the 2002 Bali bombings, and had connections with al Qaeda leaders.

2007-06-14.title: An Unexpected Odd Couple: Free Markets and Freedom
2007-06-14.abstract: The success of free market autocracies is leading intellectuals to back away from the notion that capitalism and democracy need each other to survive.

2007-06-14.title: 70 Million Years Ago, Birdlike Giant in China
2007-06-14.abstract: Scientists have uncovered the fossil skeleton of a robust birdlike dinosaur that lived 70 million years ago.

2007-06-14.title: Anti-Syrian Lawmaker Killed in Lebanon Blast
2007-06-14.abstract: A massive bomb exploded at a popular seaside club in Beirut, killing a prominent lawmaker and nine other people.

2007-06-15.title: Rebels With a Cause
2007-06-15.abstract: Jeffrey Gettleman travels with the Ogaden National Liberation Front, a  separatist rebel movement in eastern Ethiopia.

2007-06-15.title: Russian Military Officers Are Convicted of Killing 6 Chechens
2007-06-15.abstract: Four officers were convicted in their third trial for multiple killings in Chechnya. They were given sentences of 9 to 14 years.

2007-06-15.title: Kurt Waldheim, Former U.N. Chief, Is Dead at 88
2007-06-15.abstract: Kurt Waldheim was also the former president of Austria whose hidden ties to Nazi organizations were exposed late in his career.

2007-06-15.title: U.S. to Keep Europe as Site for Missile Defense
2007-06-15.abstract: The move comes despite an unexpected proposal by President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia to use a radar base in Azerbaijan instead.

2007-06-15.title: Palestinian Split Deepens; Government in Chaos
2007-06-15.abstract: With Hamas tightening its control of the Gaza Strip amid more bloodshed, the Palestinian territories seemed headed toward a turbulent political divide.

2007-06-16.title: Canada: Report Critical of ‘Broken’ Mounties
2007-06-16.abstract: The head of an investigation into the Royal Canadian Mounted Police, David A. Brown, said in a report that the force’s management and operations structure was “horribly broken.” Mr. Brown, the former head of the Ontario Securities Commission, was asked by the government to examine how the force had handled allegations that money was misspent by administrators of the pension and insurance fund. He concluded that officers who raised questions had been punished, that those responsible were not held to account and that an ostensibly independent investigation by Ottawa’s municipal police force, which found no wrongdoing, was effectively guided by the Mounties. He was particularly critical of the “autocratic leadership style” of the Mounties’ former commissioner, Giuliano Zaccardelli. Mr. Zaccardelli resigned this year after retracting some of his parliamentary testimony about the case of Maher Arar, the Canadian citizen deported to Syria by the United States after the Mounties provided erroneous information suggesting that he was a terrorism suspect.

2007-06-16.title: China: Workers Told to Wear T-Shirts to Save Energy
2007-06-16.abstract: In an effort to conserve energy, the government has urged office workers to abandon business suits in favor of T-shirts and other informal wear. The message was delivered in a week when air-conditioning in many government offices was turned off and inspectors sent to hotels and office buildings in Beijing to enforce new temperature guidelines.

2007-06-16.title: Pakistani Judge’s Lawyers Are Confident of Winning His Reinstatement
2007-06-16.abstract: Lawyers representing the suspended chief justice, Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry, said they could secure his reinstatement by the end of the month.

2007-06-16.title: Building a TV Station and a Platform for Leftists
2007-06-16.abstract: Andrés Izarra leads Telesur, a regional Spanish-language network financed largely by Venezuela’s government and described as Latin America’s answer to Al Jazeera.

2007-06-16.title: In Iraq, Gates Says Progress Toward Peace Is Lagging
2007-06-16.abstract: The U.S. defense secretary bluntly expressed his disappointment with the pace of political reconciliation under Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki.

2007-06-17.title: Body of Iraqi Newspaper Editor Found in Morgue
2007-06-17.abstract: The editor of Al Sabah was kidnapped three days ago in a Shiite neighborhood of Baghdad.

2007-06-17.title: 5 Die in Kabul Suicide Blast; U.S. Gunfire Kills Afghan
2007-06-17.abstract: American soldiers fired into a crowd near the scene of the blast, accidentally killing one man and wounding another.

2007-06-17.title: Abbas Moves to Reassert Fatah Authority
2007-06-17.abstract: The Palestinian president formed a new government, reasserting his authority in the West Bank after Hamas seized power in Gaza.

2007-06-17.title: Sarkozy’s Party Wins Smaller Majority Than Expected in French Assembly
2007-06-17.abstract: The outcome of the French parliamentary elections was a net gain of seats for the left.

2007-06-17.title: General Says Prison Inquiry Led to His Forced Retirement
2007-06-17.abstract: Gen. Antonio M. Taguba, who investigated abuse at the Abu Ghraib prison, has said that in early 2006 he was ordered, without explanation, to retire within a year.

2007-06-18.title: Behind the Che Bandannas, Shades of Potential Militias
2007-06-18.abstract: Visitors to the Alexis Vive Collective, one of the radical pro-Chávez groups that thrive in the slums of Caracas, Venezuela, can look for the mural of Jesus with an assault rifle.

2007-06-18.title: Indonesian Terror Group Limits Attacks
2007-06-18.abstract: Long before the arrests of two of its top militants last week, Jemaah Islamiyah had been moving away from the tactic of large-scale attacks, experts said Sunday.

2007-06-18.title: Sudan and U.N. Reach New Peacekeeping Deal for Darfur
2007-06-18.abstract: The proposal to send more than 20,000 peacekeeping troops to Darfur, clears the way for a joint force with the African Union, led and paid for by the United Nations.

2007-06-18.title: French Conservatives Win; Socialists Make Gains
2007-06-18.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy’s governing party won a solid victory in parliamentary elections on Sunday, but in a surprise, it failed to trounce the opposition on the left.

2007-06-18.title: Backing Abbas, U.S. Ends Embargo on Palestinian Aid
2007-06-18.abstract: The decision freed up tens of millions of dollars in aid that has been frozen since the Hamas victory in legislative elections in early 2006.

2007-06-19.title: Italy: Search for Immigrants Whose Vessel Sank
2007-06-19.abstract: The Italian Coast Guard continued its search for bodies after a vessel carrying immigrants sank off the southern Italian island of Lampedusa over the weekend. So far 11 of the 14 bodies they saw have been recovered, according to a Coast Guard spokesman who said the nationality of the victims remained unknown. Lampedusa, south of Sicily, is one of the main destinations of immigrants seeking to enter Europe from across the Mediterranean.

2007-06-19.title: The Netherlands: Milosevic Aide Faces Trial
2007-06-19.abstract: Vlastimir Djordjevic, an aide to the former Yugoslav leader Slobodan Milosevic, will appear in court in The Hague today to face charges of murder and persecution. Mr. Djordjevic, who was unexpectedly handed over to the war crimes tribunal on Sunday, had been living in Russia. He was arrested in Montenegro, where the police said he had arrived under a false identity. Mr. Djordjevic led Serbia’s security police during the 1999 Kosovo war. Prosecutors say he had a major role in deporting Kosovo Albanians. His arrest leaves four others on the run.

2007-06-19.title: United Nations to Help Lebanon Investigation
2007-06-19.abstract: The Security Council approved a request from Lebanon’s prime minister to have the United Nations commission investigating the 2005 assassination of the former Lebanese prime minister, Rafik Hariri, help investigate last week’s killing of a legislator, Walid Eido. Mr. Eido died in an attack similar to the one that had killed Mr. Hariri, when a vehicle parked along the same road in Beirut blew up as his car passed. Like Mr. Hariri, Mr. Eido was opposed to past Syrian domination of Lebanon’s politics. Preliminary findings of the commission have implicated Syrian intelligence services in Mr. Hariri’s murder.

2007-06-19.title: French Socialists’ First Couple Disclose a Parting of Ways
2007-06-19.abstract: After an election loss, France’s Socialist Party was struggling to adjust to the news that its first couple — Ségolène Royal and François Hollande — had split up.

2007-06-19.title: Desperation Rises at Closed Border Crossings Between Gaza Strip and Israel
2007-06-19.abstract: Several hundred people, most of them Fatah supporters, have been camped out at the closed border between Israel and Gaza, saying they fear for their lives.

2007-06-20.title: U.S. Seeks to Block Exits for Iraq Insurgents
2007-06-20.abstract: American forces have been confounded by insurgents who have slipped away only to fight another day.

2007-06-20.title: European Parliament: You Call That Vodka?
2007-06-20.abstract: European Parliament legislators offended vodka purists by backing a proposed definition of vodka that says it must be made from grain or potatoes.

2007-06-20.title: Thai Government Orders Ousted Leader to Return to Face Charges
2007-06-20.abstract: Thailand’s military government ordered Thaksin Shinawatra, the man it ousted from power, to return to the country or face arrest on charges of concealing assets.

2007-06-20.title: Karzai Cites Taliban Shift to Terror Attacks
2007-06-20.abstract: The Afghan president said that recent suicide bombings were driven by frustration with a flagging insurgency and the increasing strength of the Afghan Army.

2007-06-20.title: Gazans Adjust to Power Shift as New Rulers Revel in Their Victory
2007-06-20.abstract: In post-revolutionary Gaza, trashed and looted, Hamas’s victory has left many feeling vulnerable and afraid.

2007-06-21.title: General Strike Over Rising Fuel Price Takes Hold in Nigerian Cities
2007-06-21.abstract: The strike by powerful labor unions is President Umaru Yar’Adua’s first major challenge since taking office three weeks ago.

2007-06-21.title: U.S. Is Urging Blair to Be Lead Mideast Envoy
2007-06-21.abstract: If the proposal goes forward, Tony Blair would represent the “quartet” of world powers in talks between Israelis and Palestinians.

2007-06-21.title: Shiite Rivalries Slash at a Once Calm Iraqi City
2007-06-21.abstract: Unrest in Diwaniya reflects a new landscape in which Shiite groups no longer look to politics to allocate power.

2007-06-21.title: Egypt Arranges Peace Summit in Support of Abbas
2007-06-21.abstract: Closing ranks against Hamas, Egypt has invited Israeli, Palestinian and Jordanian leaders to a summit next week.

2007-06-21.title: U.S. Holds Direct Talks in North Korea on Arms
2007-06-21.abstract: In the first visit by a senior U.S. official in nearly five years, America’s chief nuclear negotiator was in Pyongyang today in an effort to speed up nuclear negotiations.

2007-06-22.title: Japan: Abe Delays National Election
2007-06-22.abstract: Prime Minister Shinzo Abe delayed the critical upper house election by a week, to July 29 from July 22, by extending Parliament’s current session by 12 days, in what was seen as a desperate move to regain popular support and dampen widespread anger over the government’s admission that it had misplaced payment information for millions of pensioners. Polls show that support for Mr. Abe and his Liberal Democratic Party has fallen dangerously low among potential voters, with the main opposition Democratic Party scoring higher in some polls. A significant defeat could put pressure on Mr. Abe to step down as party leader and prime minister, though his party would keep the prime minister’s post through its control over the lower house.

2007-06-22.title: U.S. Troops Find Buried Dangers at Outpost
2007-06-22.abstract: In trying to bring insurgent- controlled areas around Baghdad under control, U.S. troops have found dozens of bombs buried beneath the road, some big enough to destroy an Abrams tank.

2007-06-22.title: Afghans Say NATO Bombs Kill 25 Civilians
2007-06-22.abstract: The civilians were caught in a battle between Taliban and NATO forces in southern Afghanistan, the police said.

2007-06-22.title: Pakistan: Like Rushdie, a Lofty Title for bin Laden
2007-06-22.abstract: A group of clerics, the Pakistan Ulema Council, said they had given Osama bin Laden the title Saifullah, or sword of Allah, in response to the British government’s decision to give Salman Rushdie, the author of “The Satanic Verses,” a knighthood. The chairman of the council, Tahir Ashrafi, said that since Mr. Rushdie, who is considered a blasphemer by many Muslims in Pakistan, had been given the title “Sir,” then Mr. bin Laden, a holy warrior in the eyes of the council, should be given a parallel title. The gesture was a more lighthearted reaction to the honor for Mr. Rushdie than the noisy protests of demonstrators in some cities of Pakistan. On Monday, Pakistan’s Parliament passed a resolution condemning the knighthood and said it should be withdrawn.

2007-06-22.title: Might Drink Your Blood, but Otherwise Not Bad Guys
2007-06-22.abstract: The mystery of the Mungiki, a secret society that is part Sicilian Mafia, part Chicago street gang, is sweeping across Kenya and taking a lot of lives with it.

2007-06-23.title: Chirac Refuses to Testify in French Dirty Tricks Case
2007-06-23.abstract: Having lost the immunity that protected him from prosecution while in office, Jacques Chirac, the former French president, has refused to testify about a scandal.

2007-06-23.title: Water Woes in Irish City Are Downside of Growth
2007-06-23.abstract: Residents of Galway have been boiling drinking water since March, when health authorities linked a nasty stomach ailment to a parasite lurking in the city’s water.

2007-06-23.title: Militants Said to Flee Before U.S. Offensive
2007-06-23.abstract: The second-ranking U.S. commander in Iraq said that Qaeda leaders had been alerted to a plan for an American push in Baquba.

2007-06-23.title: Rockets Kill 9 in Pakistan Tribal Area
2007-06-23.abstract: Officials described overnight rocket attacks from across the Afghan border in the Shawal area of the North Waziristan tribal region of Pakistan.

2007-06-23.title: North Korea ‘Prepared’ to Shut Down Reactor, U.S. Envoy Says
2007-06-23.abstract: The chief American nuclear envoy, returning from a visit to North Korea, said that Pyongyang had assured him it would ultimately disable its main nuclear reactor.

2007-06-24.title: General’s Iraq Progress Report Has Competition
2007-06-24.abstract: The Bush administration will seek a range of views beyond the report by the U.S. commander in Iraq.

2007-06-24.title: Hussein Cousin Gets Death Sentence for Mass Killings
2007-06-24.abstract: Ali Hassan al-Majid, known as Chemical Ali, oversaw a campaign that killed tens of thousands of Kurds in the 1980s. Two others were also sentenced to death today.

2007-06-24.title: Israel to Transfer Millions to Abbas to Bolster Him
2007-06-24.abstract: The policy aim is the strengthen Mahmoud Abbas’s rule in what is generally regarded as the pro-Fatah West Bank.

2007-06-24.title: Eight U.S. Soldiers Are Killed in Iraq Violence
2007-06-24.abstract: Eight American servicemen and one British soldier were reported Saturday to have been killed in Iraq, bringing the four-day death toll to at least 25.

2007-06-24.title: Labor Formally Names Brown as Blair’s Successor
2007-06-24.abstract: Treasury chief Gordon Brown replaced Tony Blair as leader of the governing Labor Party on Sunday.

2007-06-25.title: Hussein Cousin Sentenced to Die for Kurd Attacks
2007-06-25.abstract: A judge gave five death sentences to the man known as Chemical Ali for his role in gas attacks on Kurdish villagers, but Iraqi interest in the trials has flagged.

2007-06-25.title: U.S. Generals Doubt Ability of Iraqi Army to Hold Gains
2007-06-25.abstract: The American commander in Baquba, Brig. Gen. Mick Bednarek, said Iraqi forces are “not quite up to the job yet.”

2007-06-25.title: Israel Will Support Abbas With Millions From Taxes
2007-06-25.abstract: Israel on Sunday agreed to transfer hundreds of millions of dollars of Palestinian tax revenues to the Palestinian president, Mahmoud Abbas of Fatah.

2007-06-25.title: Iraq Bombing Kills Sunni Sheiks Allied With U.S.
2007-06-25.abstract: A suicide bomber assassinated several Sunni Aran sheiks who were cooperating with Americans to fight Al Qaeda.

2007-06-25.title: Turkish Entry Into Europe Slowed by Sarkozy Move
2007-06-25.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy of France blocked a key element of Turkey’s entry negotiations with the European Union today.

2007-06-26.title: For G.I.’s in Iraq, a Harrowing Day Facing a Trap
2007-06-26.abstract: The soldiers from Comanche Company’s First Platoon faced a network of houses rigged to explode by insurgents.

2007-06-26.title: U.N. Inspectors Invited to Iran for Talks on Nuclear Program
2007-06-26.abstract: A team of inspectors from the International Atomic Energy Agency will travel to Tehran in the coming weeks to “develop an action plan,” the agency said Monday.

2007-06-26.title: Egypt: Engineer Gets Life on Israeli Spy Charge
2007-06-26.abstract: A court in Cairo convicted Muhammad Sayyid Saber Ali, an Egyptian nuclear engineer, of spying for Israel and sentenced him to life in prison.

2007-06-26.title: Murdoch’s Dealings in China: It’s Business, and It’s Personal
2007-06-26.abstract: Many companies have tried to break into the Chinese market, but few have been as ardent and unrelenting as Rupert Murdoch’s News Corporation.

2007-06-26.title: U.S. Plan to Capture and Kill Insurgents in Baquba Fell Far Short of Goal, Officer Says
2007-06-26.abstract: One week after the U.S. assault on insurgent strongholds in Baquba, at least half of the estimated 300 to 500 fighters who were there have escaped or are still at large.

2007-06-27.title: Canada: Native Protests Planned for National Day
2007-06-27.abstract: Native groups are threatening to cause major travel disruptions at the start of the forthcoming Canada Day holiday weekend to protest poverty and land claims.

2007-06-27.title: Raid on Top Sunni Official Adds to Iraqi Internal Feuding
2007-06-27.abstract: An arrest warrant accused the Iraqi culture minister of masterminding the attempted assassination of a member of Parliament, who was once a top aide to Ahmad Chalabi.

2007-06-27.title: Tooth May Have Solved Mummy Mystery
2007-06-27.abstract: A single tooth and some DNA clues appear to have “positively identified” the mummy of Hatshepsut, a great queen of ancient Egypt, who reigned in the 15th century B.C.

2007-06-27.title: Fuel Rationing in Iran Prompts Protests
2007-06-27.abstract: Protesters set fire to at least two gas stations in Tehran last night after the announcement of the fuel rationing.

2007-06-27.title: France: Teenager Dies on Disneyland Ride
2007-06-27.abstract: French police were investigating the death of a 14-year-old Spanish girl on the fast Rock ’n’ Roller Coaster ride at the Disneyland just outside Paris.

2007-06-28.title: Blair to Tackle Economics but Not Peace Efforts, a Task Reserved for Rice
2007-06-28.abstract: In his new role as envoy to the Middle East, Tony Blair will be charged with shoring up Palestinian institutions but not with trying to nail down a peace deal.

2007-06-28.title: 20 Decapitated Bodies Found in Iraq
2007-06-28.abstract: The grisly discovery in a mostly Sunni village was made on a bloody day in Iraq, as a car bomb attack killed 25.

2007-06-28.title: Israelis Kill 11 Militants Inside Gaza; 2 Civilians Die
2007-06-28.abstract: At least 11 Palestinian militants and 2 civilians were killed in Gaza in the deadliest day since the militant group Hamas took control.

2007-06-28.title: Pentagon Splits War-Support Contract 3 Ways
2007-06-28.abstract: The Army has awarded contracts to feed, fuel and house American troops, breaking the job into three deals rather than giving most of the work to one company.

2007-06-28.title: Wider Use of Latin Mass Likely, Vatican Officials Say
2007-06-28.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI has signed a document that would allow more churches to adopt the old Latin Mass that largely faded from use during the 1960s.

2007-06-29.title: 5 U.S. Soldiers Killed in Iraq Attack
2007-06-29.abstract: The soldiers killed today were conducting an operation aimed at reducing sectarian violence, the military said.

2007-06-29.title: Stoic Londoners Shrug at Latest Threat
2007-06-29.abstract: Many people in the streets near Piccadilly Circus seemed less than troubled here today about the discovery of an explosive device.

2007-06-29.title: Colombian Rebels Blamed for Hostage Deaths
2007-06-29.abstract: A report complicates recent efforts to thaw relations between the rebels and Colombia’s government, which have fought for four decades.

2007-06-29.title: Kiichi Miyazawa, Japan Premier in the ’90s, Dies at 87
2007-06-29.abstract: The former prime minister and holder of many other top government posts helped guide Japan from postwar ruin to economic superpower.

2007-06-29.title: Sectarian Attacks Kill Dozens in Baghdad
2007-06-29.abstract: The attacks were days ahead of a planned march of devout Shiites through Sunni heartlands to the remnants of a revered shrine.

2007-06-30.title: Office That Sought Forbidden Iraqi Weapons Is Shut Down
2007-06-30.abstract: The Security Council voted to shut down the U.N. units that had searched for biological, chemical and nuclear weapons in Iraq before the United States-led invasion.

2007-06-30.title: Nigeria: President Says He’s Worth $5 Million
2007-06-30.abstract: In an effort to turn a page on a reputation for entrenched government corruption, Nigeria’s new president, Umaru Yar’Adua, has taken the unusual step of declaring his net worth.

2007-06-30.title: Saving Wildlife in Zambia, and Raising Human Prospects
2007-06-30.abstract: Describing Hammer Simwinga’s work is easy: He shows rural Zambians that there are better ways to get ahead than by killing animals. Doing it well is another matter.

2007-06-30.title: Ivory Coast Premier Survives Attack on His Plane in a Rebel Area
2007-06-30.abstract: Ivory Coast’s prime minister was unhurt, but three people  were killed when his plane came under heavy fire as it landed in the northern rebel stronghold of Bouaké.

2007-06-30.title: London Finds Linked Bombs, a Qaeda Tactic
2007-06-30.abstract: Two Mercedes sedans filled with gasoline, nails and gas canisters had been parked near Piccadilly Circus in the bustling West End.
